Thu, May 29, 2025 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM CDT

Axelrad Beer Garden

1517 Alabama Street · Houston, TX

Report Ad
dbt Global Circuit Series: Houston
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Houston dbt Meetup
2 reviews
Details

In this meetup, we’ll dive into the latest tools and ideas we’ve been shipping — what they unlock, how they fit together, and why we built them the way we did.

This meetup is designed for builders, thinkers, and tinkerers — folks who want to roll up their sleeves (figuratively), learn from each other, and imagine what’s next for the analytics engineering stack.

Whether you’re curious about what’s new, want to see how others are approaching similar problems, or just want to ask hard questions — you’re in the right place.

🤝Organizer: dbt Labs (Paul Romo)
🏠Venue Host: Axelrad Beer Garden @ 1517 Alabama St, Houston, TX 77004

To attend, please read the Health and Safety Policy and Terms of Participation:** https://www.getdbt.com/legal/health-and-safety-policy
📝Agenda

Check in/Registration
Presentation: For Builders, by Builders: The Latest Tools from dbt Labs” by Katherine Brock (Sr Solutions Architect, dbt Labs) (20-25 min)
Presentation Q&A (5 min)
Networking and Reception (1 hour)

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 6pm.
Parking:Find a free parking lot behind Luigi's that Axelrad shares with them and Retrospect. There are two more paid lots across the street and catty corner from the front door.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝 Make sure to join the #local-texas channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).

***

dbt is the standard in data transformation, used by over 50,000 organizations worldwide. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust. Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more

·
Host

Thank you for registering for our upcoming meetup!

To make sure everyone gets the most out of the experience—especially the networking opportunities—we’ve decided to postpone the meetup until we have a few more participants. Our goal is to create a vibrant and valuable event for everyone involved.

We'll be in touch soon with a new date. In the meantime, feel free to spread the word or invite others who might be interested in joining us.

Thanks for your understanding and support—we look forward to seeing you soon!

Best, Amada

events.page_title: Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs), Thu, Nov 21, 2024, 1:00 PM | Meetup
events.raw_text:

Thu, Nov 21, 2024 · 8:00 PM to 9:00 PM CET

Online event

This event has passed

Report Ad
Community AMA with Erica Louie (dbt Labs)
Network event
D

Hosted by dbt L.

Super Organizer

Houston dbt Meetup
2 reviews
Details

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Erica Louie, Data @ dbt Labs
Host: Bolaji Oyejide, Digital Community Mgr @ dbt Labs
Topic: Careers in data, building a data team, the dbt Semantic Layer, and scaling your dbt project
When: November 21st @ 2pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Erica was the second employee at dbt Labs, then called Fishtown Analytics and she's seen dbt grow, a lot. In this AMA, Erica will share more on her career in data, her strong opinions on building a data team, how she manages her team, the dbt Semantic Layer, scaling your dbt project, and more on her Substack.

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-ama channel in Slack to participate live!

Read more
Speaker
Erica Louie, dbt Labs

Second hire at dbt Labs, former Head of Data, and now leading the product data team (and previously worked for BuzzFeed's data team), Erica "ric" Louie proudly hails from a liberal arts background (studied political science + italian) and grew her career in data at dbt Labs starting from the ground-up. She firmly believes that anyone can be a data practitioner with the right mentors, resources (sans trivial data jargon), and a good sense of humor. While rethinking the philosophy behind data systems, operations, and teams is one of her favorite topics, she enjoys thinking and writing about the human experience, and how it intersects with the work we choose to do and who we choose to do it with.

REGISTER HERE 👉 https://www.getdbt.com/resources/webinars/community-slack-ama

🚀 Hey Community! Are you ready to dive deep, get insights, and satisfy your curiosity?

We’re hosting monthly live Ask Me Anything events! It's the perfect platform to quench your curiosity and get to know each other better. Don't miss out on this opportunity to connect and learn together.

What to Expect:

Discussing trends in the analytics space
Sharing key takeaways from the recent events
Hearing your questions and thoughts

Speaker: Alex Welch, Head of Data @dbt Labs
Topic: BI Tools & Semantic Layer
When: June 27 @ 4pm EST
Where: Virtual and in #dbt-community-merge

Whether you’re looking at flight data, sales figures, consumer transactions, or biometric stats — access to the correct sources + a well-trained user + BI tools + dbt = data that works!!
This month we have Alex Welch, Head of Data at dbt Labs, to answer questions and discuss more about BI tools, analytics trends & the Semantic Layer!

Register now to get the link to watch and join the #dbt-community-merge channel in Slack to participate live!

Wed, Aug 16, 2023 · 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM CDT

Buffalo Bayou Brewing Company

2101 Summer St · Houston, TX

Report Ad
Houston dbt Meetup - Buffalo Bayou Brewing
S

Hosted by Savannah W.

Houston dbt Meetup
2 reviews
Details

dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.

🏠Venue Host: Buffalo Bayou Brewing, 2101 Summer Street Houston TX, 77007
🍕Catering: Buffalo Bayou Brewing
🤝Organizer: Jason Small, Savannah Cole, and Emily Huffman

To attend, please read the Required Participation Language for In-Person Events with dbt Labs: https://bit.ly/3QIJXFb

📝Agenda
5:00 - 5:30 pm | Check in/Registration (30 min)
5:45 - 5:50 pm | Welcome Remarks by Meetup Host (5 min)
5:50 - 6:10 pm | Presentation (30 min)
6:10 - 6:15 pm | Presentation Q&A (5 min)
6:15 - 6:20 pm| Closing Remarks by Meetup Host and Call for speakers for next time
6:30 - 7:30 pm | Reception

🗣️Presentation: How to Leverage dbt for Data Science Workflows

EVENT DETAILS:
The doors open at 5pm. Presentations begin at 5:45pm. Food and refreshments will be provided.

Our venue has capacity limits, so please only RSVP if you intend to come and and reach out to emily.r.huffman@kipi.bi or DM in dbt Slack if you need to cancel last minute or change your RSVP status on the Meetup to "Not Going."

DIRECTIONS: Buffalo Bayou Brewing has a parking lot to accommodate guests. When you arrive you may ask to be directed to the Sunset Room where the event is being held.

➡️ Join the dbt Slack community: https://www.getdbt.com/community/
🤝For the best Meetup experience, make sure to join the #local-\ channel in dbt Slack (https://slack.getdbt.com/).
----------------------------------
dbt is a data transformation framework that lets analysts and engineers collaborate using their shared knowledge of SQL. Through the application of software engineering best practices like modularity, version control, testing, and documentation, dbt’s analytics engineering workflow helps teams work more efficiently to produce data the entire organization can trust.

Learn more: https://www.getdbt.com/

Read more
Speaker
Alex Abraham

I'm a Data Scientist, interested in data products' full stack: from data engineering to model deployment. I eagerly use dbt in the data foundation of my product concept for baseball analytics. Historically, I've focused on economic modeling: price elasticities for a large home improvement retailer, credit risk for a fintech startup, and Texas job growth with the Federal Reserve. I studied Statistics at Texas A&M University and Economics at Purdue University.

Connect with Alex: https://www.linkedin.com/in/alex-abraham-a345aab2/
